claim: Odigo, an Israeli instant-messaging company, reported that two of its employees in its Herzliya, Israel office received English-language instant messages approximately two hours before the September 11 attacks, warning that 'something big' was going to happen — messages reportedly ending in an antisemitic slur. Odigo provided the originating IP address to the FBI.

sources.label: Ha'aretz — “Odigo Says Workers Were Warned of Attack” (Sep 26, 2001)
sources.quote: two of its workers received messages two hours before the Twin Towers attack on September 11 predicting the attack would happen

sources.label: Ha'aretz — “Two Odigo Workers Received Messages Predicting WTC Attack” (Sep 25, 2001)
Computerworld — “FBI probes advance IM warnings on attacks”
Haley Press / @strategichaley, Instagram reel DVOtv6QCTFf (Feb 26, 2026) — surfacing source

notes: Ha'aretz coverage is the contemporaneous primary news report. URLs not yet directly fetched in this pass. The FBI investigation outcome is not in the public record. The conspiratorial framing — that the warning constitutes Israeli foreknowledge — is contested; the existence of the messages and the company's report to the FBI are documented.
